metadata.title:
RAIL Way OFFICES WILL


MOVE TO NORTHPORT

pages.raw_text:
There is no longer an doubt con-
corning the moving of the Spokane
Falls & Northern general Offices from
Spokane to Northport as first an
nounced by The Press.


r. c. Morgan, the newly appointed
superintendent, just returned from a
trip to Northport, where he was look
ing over the depot at that point with
view of having portion of it made
into offices for the roadmasters. An
other building has been selected for
the offices of the various departments
and the transfer of the S. +. & n.
Offices will be made within a month,
or as soon as the building at North
port is put in shape.


Superintendent Morgan has already
made some changes in the operation
of the trains on the Marcus Republic
line and more will follow with a view
of reducing the operating expenses.
Mr.. Morgan is of the Hill school and
is a shrewd railway man.


The Spokane Falls & Northern rail-
way will establish a train dispatcher's
